behavior_summary: Implements activation and deactivation logic for a sliding/toggle floor item: when triggered it plays a sound, inspects the item's current animation frame and type, and either attempts a placement/move (via FREE::2411) or advances/retreats the item's animation frame and schedules a short delayed follow-up.

The primary handler (Func_80) is the activation path: if the item frame != 4 it increments the frame, writes it back, and spawns a short-lived delayed process (FREE::1C19) to finalize or visually update the state. If the frame == 4 it gathers item coordinates and type, maps type -> index, and calls FREE::2411 to attempt a move/placement; its return value gates continuation.

A complementary helper (Func_1A1) mirrors the main flow for the opposite direction: it plays the same sound, maps type with a mirrored order, calls FREE::2411, then decrements the frame and conditionally spawns the same delayed process when appropriate.
notable_control_flow: Initial always-run actions: set-info (engine op inferred) and playSFX at entry, then a suspend/wait.
Primary decision: branch on Item::getFrame() == 4 — this selects either the placement/move path (FREE::2411) or the frame-advance path (increment frame and schedule delayed update).
Type-based mapping: Item::getType() is compared against constants to select a mappedIndex passed into FREE::2411; this changes behavior per slider variant.
FREE::2411 return value acts as a guard: its result determines whether the function returns/aborts early or continues to update frames and spawn follow-ups.
Frame changes are accompanied by spawning FREE::1C19(pid, 0x0A, 0) to schedule a visual/finalizing update after a short delay.
open_questions: Exact semantics of FREE::2411: inferred to be a placement/movement helper but its precise effects, meaning of the mappedIndex parameter, and its return-value contract are unknown.
Purpose and behavior of spawned FREE::1C19: appears to schedule a visual update or finalize a placement, but exact payload and how it interacts with the item remain unclear.
Meaning of the numeric frame threshold (== 4): whether this corresponds to a fully retracted/extended state depends on the animation conventions used elsewhere.
Details of the type-to-index mapping constants: what each mappedIndex identifies (direction, shape variant, or movement mode) is inferred but not certain.
Any global side-effects of the 'set info' and implied engine ops at function entry (what info key is being set and why) are only inferred.

main_function.name: Func_80 (SLIDER::0080)
main_function.purpose: Handle the slider's activation trigger: play activation sound, inspect and update the item's animation frame or attempt a placement/move based on frame/type, and schedule a short delayed task to finalize or visually update the change.
entry_conditions: Invoked when the actor/process activates or enters the slider trigger area (inferred from playSFX and 'set info' operations).
Runs as an item/process handler that suspends briefly after playing the sound (synchronous audio/animation coupling).

function_map.name: U8P_SLIDER::Func_80 (SLIDER::0080)
function_map.role: Primary activation handler for the slider item; decides between advancing frame or invoking placement/movement.

function_map.name: U8P_SLIDER::Func_1A1 (SLIDER::01A1)
function_map.role: Complementary/deactivation handler that mirrors the main flow and performs the reverse frame change path and placement attempts.

function_map.name: Item::*
function_map.role: Provided item API used to read/write item frame, position, type, and to destroy or bark (used across handlers).

function_map.name: FREE::*
function_map.role: Engine helper processes: FREE::2411 (placement/movement helper) and FREE::1C19 (spawned short-lived follow-up); used for movement gating and delayed updates.

function_map.name: U8P_SLIDER::playSFX
function_map.role: Play a sound effect to provide audio feedback on activation/deactivation.

external_calls.purpose: Play slider activation sound immediately when the handler runs; provides audio feedback for the action.
Read the item's current animation/frame to decide whether to take the placement/move path or to advance the frame.
Write the incremented frame when advancing the slider animation.
Retrieve item's X coordinate for use by FREE::2411 in placement/move attempt.
Retrieve item's Y coordinate for use by FREE::2411 in placement/move attempt.
Retrieve item's Z (level/height) for use by FREE::2411 in placement/move attempt.
Read item type so that the code can map it to a mappedIndex that parametrizes FREE::2411 behavior (variant-specific behavior).
Attempt a placement/movement operation using (z,y,x, mode byte, mappedIndex, item pointer, indirect data). Its return value gates whether frame updates/scheduling proceed.
Spawn a short-lived delayed process (pid, delay 0x0A, arg 0) to finalize or visually update the slider after a brief delay following a frame change.
Play the same slider sound for the complementary/deactivation path.
Same placement/move attempt as the main function but with a mirrored mappedIndex/mode; its success/failure affects whether the frame is decremented and whether follow-up is scheduled.
Write the decremented frame when retreating/deactivating the slider animation.
Conditionally spawn the delayed follow-up when the decremented frame remains non-zero.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Implements activation and deactivation logic for a sliding/toggle floor item: when triggered it plays a sound, inspects the item's current animation frame and type, and either attempts a placement/move (via FREE::2411) or advances/retreats the item's animation frame and schedules a short delayed follow-up.
